title: iris graminea var. sylvatica
intro: Iris graminea var. sylvatica, also known as the wild iris or small-leaved iris, is a plant species in the iris family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Iris graminea var. sylvatica include its potential to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in homeopathic medicine.
